summary: Complete succession resolution for Alpha playthroughs
acceptance_criteria: M3 succession state machine is completed for Alpha playthroughs with deterministic candidate generation, eligibility, claim strength, legitimacy effects, and auditable resolution events.

Succession completion handles deaths, abdication-like scenario triggers, disputed succession, regency or temporary stewardship if accepted by the M6 contract, and recovery from malformed inputs without data corruption.
All succession commands, queries, events, and save fields are explicit, versioned, serializable schemas with stable IDs and no object-graph serialization.
Succession consequences connect to diplomacy, legitimacy, offices, vassal obligations, and start-to-victory outcomes without duplicating those systems.
required_tests: Property/invariant tests cover stable heir ordering, no cyclic title/claim references, disputed succession reason codes, save round-trip, and malformed/future payload rejection.
notes: Keep succession deterministic and data-driven; no real-time or object traversal order assumptions.
Historical uncertainty in succession rules must come from scenario pipeline labels, not hard-coded certainty.
Stop if the design requires formal historical content beyond the accepted scenario pipeline review.
